name: alembic
description: Alembic is a lightweight database migration tool for SQLAlchemy, providing schema versioning, autogeneration of migrations, and branching support

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialize a new Alembic environment, creating the migrations directory structure, alembic.ini configuration, and env.py script.

args.name: directory
args.description: Name of the migration directory to create (e.g., alembic, migrations)

options.name: --template
options.description: Template to use for the environment (generic, multidb, async, pylons)

options.name: --package
options.description: Create __init__.py in the migration directory to make it a Python package

subcommands.name: revision
subcommands.description: Create a new revision (migration) file. Supports both empty revisions and autogenerated ones based on model comparison.

options.name: --message
options.description: Message string for the revision

options.name: --autogenerate
options.description: Auto-detect schema changes by comparing models with the database

options.name: --head
options.description: Head revision to use as the parent (default: head)

options.name: --splice
options.description: Allow a non-head revision as the parent, creating a branch

options.name: --branch-label
options.description: Label for a new branch from this revision

options.name: --version-path
options.description: Directory to store the revision file

options.name: --rev-id
options.description: Custom revision identifier

options.name: --depends-on
options.description: Revision(s) this depends on

options.name: --sql
options.description: Produce SQL script instead of running the migration

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Apply migration(s) to upgrade the database schema to a target revision. Can upgrade to the latest, a specific revision, or a relative number.

args.name: revision
args.description: Target revision (head, revision ID, or relative like +2)

options.name: --sql
options.description: Output SQL statements instead of executing them

options.name: --tag
options.description: Tag to pass to the migration environment

subcommands.name: downgrade
subcommands.description: Revert migration(s) to downgrade the database schema to a previous revision or by a relative number of steps.

args.name: revision
args.description: Target revision (base, revision ID, or relative like -1)

options.name: --sql
options.description: Output SQL statements instead of executing them

options.name: --tag
options.description: Tag to pass to the migration environment

subcommands.name: current
subcommands.description: Display the current revision(s) active in the database. Shows which migration the database is currently at.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show detailed revision info including path and docstring

subcommands.name: heads
subcommands.description: Show the head revision(s) of the migration tree. Multiple heads indicate unmerged branches.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show detailed revision info

options.name: --resolve-dependencies
options.description: Include dependency information in output

subcommands.name: history
subcommands.description: Show the full history of revisions from oldest to newest. Useful for understanding the migration chain and branches.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show detailed revision info for each entry

options.name: --rev-range
options.description: Limit history to a specific range (e.g., base:head, abc123:def456)

options.name: --indicate-current
options.description: Mark the current database revision in the output

subcommands.name: branches
subcommands.description: Show all branch points in the migration graph where the revision history has diverged.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show detailed revision info

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Display details of a specific revision including its revision ID, parent, description, and file path.

subcommands.name: merge
subcommands.description: Create a merge revision that combines two or more branch heads into a single revision, resolving branch divergence.

args.name: revisions
args.description: Two or more revision identifiers to merge (space-separated)

options.name: --message
options.description: Message for the merge revision

options.name: --branch-label
options.description: Label for the merged branch

options.name: --rev-id
options.description: Custom revision identifier for the merge

examples: alembic merge abc123 def456 -m 'merge feature and hotfix'

subcommands.name: stamp
subcommands.description: Set the alembic_version table to a specific revision without running any migration scripts. Useful for marking an already-migrated database.

args.name: revision
args.description: Revision to stamp (head, base, or specific ID)

options.name: --sql
options.description: Output SQL statements instead of executing them

options.name: --purge
options.description: Delete all entries from version table before stamping

options.name: --tag
options.description: Tag to pass to the migration environment

subcommands.name: edit
subcommands.description: Open a specific revision file in the system editor for manual editing.

subcommands.name: check
subcommands.description: Check if there are any new schema changes that have not been versioned as migrations. Returns exit code 0 if in sync, 1 if changes detected.

subcommands.name: ensure_version
subcommands.description: Ensure the alembic_version table exists in the database without applying any migrations.

options.name: --sql
options.description: Output SQL statements instead of executing them

subcommands.name: list_templates
subcommands.description: List all available Alembic environment templates that can be used with the init command.

globalOptions.name: --config
globalOptions.description: Path to the Alembic configuration file (default: alembic.ini)

globalOptions.name: --name
globalOptions.description: Name of the section in the config file to use for Alembic config

globalOptions.name: --raiseerr
globalOptions.description: Raise a full stack trace on error instead of a short message

globalOptions.name: -x
globalOptions.description: Additional arguments consumed by env.py (e.g., -x dbname=mydb)

detectors.name: alembic_env_detect
detectors.description: Detect Alembic env.py and migration directory

detectors.name: alembic_current_revision
detectors.description: Get the current Alembic revision in the database

detectors.name: alembic_package_detect
detectors.description: Detect alembic in Python requirements or setup files
